{{Infobox group
| name = Akura clan
| introduced = cradle-04-skysworn
}}

''See also: [[Akura clan Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|The [[Akura]] clan is a massive and immensely wealthy power that holds authority over vast territories, including the [[Blackflame Empire]]. Known for their use of [[shadow madra]], the clan possesses a reputation for being intimidating, cold, and highly resourceful. Their influence is so great that they own approximately three-quarters of the continent, and their subjects often live or die based on the family's favor. The clan is characterized by a philosophy of harshness intended to ensure human survival against threats like [[dragons]]. Members of the head family are traditionally named after virtues, such as [[Charity]], [[Malice]], [[Justice]], [[Harmony]], [[Mercy]], [[Pride]], and [[Fury]]. The clan's leadership includes powerful figures like the [[Monarch Malice]] and the [[Sage of the Silver Heart]], [[Akura Charity]]. The family maintains its strength through elite combatants, including [[Heralds]], [[Sages]], and Archlords, some of whom possess power comparable to [[Heralds]]. They also utilize a unique bloodline legacy involving specialized armor and the '[[Path of Seven Pages]],' which involves testing descendants through dream-world trials via sacred Books. The clan's crest features a mountain range topped by three stars, and their capital is located in [[Moongrave]].}}

== Skysworn ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|As owners of three-quarters of the continent, the [[Akura]] clan employs the world's most talented dream artists. These dream-readers possess the ability to observe shifts in [[fate]] and the currents of destiny. While they have shielded the [[Empire]] from [[dragons]] for generations, the clan remains largely apathetic toward the [[Blackflame Empire]] beyond their shared humanity. Their recent activities include providing battle reports concerning encounters with [[Redmoon Hall]] and making a formal request regarding an errant daughter. Furthermore, the clan does not entirely approve of [[Mercy]]'s presence.}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|The [[Akura clan]] has provided battle reports regarding encounters with [[Redmoon Hall]].<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c11">''Skysworn'', chapter 11</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|The [[Akura]] family has shielded the [[Empire]] from [[dragons]] for generations.<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c11" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|The [[Akura]] family is apathetic toward the [[Blackflame Empire]] except for being both human.<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c11" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|The [[Akura clan]] made a request regarding an errant daughter.<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c13">''Skysworn'', chapter 13</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|The [[Akura clan]] owns three-quarters of the continent.<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c14">''Skysworn'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|The [[Akura clan]] employs the world's greatest dream artists.<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c14" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|The [[Akura clan]]'s dream-readers can see shifts in [[fate]] and the currents of destiny.<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c14" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|The [[Akura clan]] does not entirely approve of [[Mercy]]'s presence.<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c15">''Skysworn'', chapter 15</ref>}}

== Ghostwater ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-05-ghostwater|The [[Akura]] clan is a group composed of various branches, often described as an empire of children. This family provides protection to the [[Skysworn]] and offers rewards for every [[Underlord]] head taken. While their power is dwarfed by that of black [[dragons]], the clan deals with such creatures and possesses [[Harmony]]'s armor as a bloodline legacy. Members like [[Akura Mercy]] and [[Yerin]] are recognized as part of the family, an entity that should not be opposed. Despite their strength and potential involvement in upcoming competitions, the clan has kept the [[fate]] of one of its children hidden from the [[Monarch]].}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-05-ghostwater|[[Akura Mercy]] is a member of the [[Akura clan]].<ref name="cradle-05-ghostwater-c2">''Ghostwater'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-05-ghostwater|The [[Skysworn]] are under the protection of the [[Akura]] family.<ref name="cradle-05-ghostwater-c3">''Ghostwater'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-05-ghostwater|The [[Akura clan]] is a group.<ref name="cradle-05-ghostwater-c4">''Ghostwater'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-05-ghostwater|The [[Akura]] family is described as an empire of children.<ref name="cradle-05-ghostwater-c7">''Ghostwater'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-05-ghostwater|The [[Akura]] family has different branches.<ref name="cradle-05-ghostwater-c7" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-05-ghostwater|The [[Akura clan]] rewards for each [[Underlord]] head.<ref name="cradle-05-ghostwater-c7" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-05-ghostwater|The power of black [[dragons]] dwarfed the [[Akura]] family.<ref name="cradle-05-ghostwater-c9">''Ghostwater'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-05-ghostwater|[[Yerin]] and [[Mercy]] are seen as members of the [[Akura]] family.<ref name="cradle-05-ghostwater-c14">''Ghostwater'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-05-ghostwater|The [[Akura]] family is an entity that should not be opposed.<ref name="cradle-05-ghostwater-c16">''Ghostwater'', chapter 16</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-05-ghostwater|The [[Akura]] family deals with [[dragons]].<ref name="cradle-05-ghostwater-c17">''Ghostwater'', chapter 17</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-05-ghostwater|[[Lindon]] has no grudge against the [[Akura clan]].<ref name="cradle-05-ghostwater-c18">''Ghostwater'', chapter 18</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-05-ghostwater|[[Harmony]]'s armor is the bloodline legacy of the [[Akura]] family.<ref name="cradle-05-ghostwater-c18" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-05-ghostwater|The [[Akura]] family might compete in the upcoming competition.<ref name="cradle-05-ghostwater-c19">''Ghostwater'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-05-ghostwater|The [[Akura]] family hid their child's [[fate]] from the [[Monarch]].<ref name="cradle-05-ghostwater-c19" />}}

== Underlord ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|The vaunted [[Akura]] clan is a [[Monarch]] faction on the continent, renowned for their legendary pillars and mastery of [[shadow madra]]. As a group that [[King Dakata]] serves, the clan holds immense influence, as their favor can determine whether the [[Blackflame Empire]] thrives or dies. Operating from a mountain-sized fortress, they oversee treasured territories like [[Night Wheel Valley]] and the lands where [[Lindon]]'s family resides. The clan maintains its power by making its vassals compete, a practice seen when they provided a signal to [[Emperor Naru Huan]] and participated in a competition involving the [[gate]]. Following their responsibility for [[Renfei]]'s death, [[Lindon]] eventually prepares to compete on behalf of the clan alongside their favored young Underlords.}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|The [[Akura clan]] has legendary pillars.<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c2">''Underlord'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|The [[Akura clan]] is famous for their use of [[shadow madra]].<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c4">''Underlord'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|The [[Akura]] family's favor determines if the [[Blackflame Empire]] thrives or dies.<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c4" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|The [[Akura]] family took responsibility for [[Renfei]]'s death.<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c4" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|The [[Akura clan]] is described as vaunted.<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c4" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|The [[Akura]] family provided a signal to [[Emperor Naru Huan]].<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c6">''Underlord'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|The [[Akura clan]] considers [[Night Wheel Valley]] a treasured territory.<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c6" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|The [[Akura]] family makes their vassals compete.<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c6" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|The [[Akura]] family is involved in a competition involving the [[gate]].<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c7">''Underlord'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|The [[Akura clan]] has a mountain-sized fortress.<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c9">''Underlord'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|[[Lindon]] wonders what the [[Akura]] family will think of his actions.<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c9" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|The [[Akura clan]] is a [[Monarch]] faction on the continent.<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c13">''Underlord'', chapter 13</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|The [[Akura]] family has vassals.<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c15">''Underlord'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|The [[Akura clan]] is a group that [[King Dakata]] serves.<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c16">''Underlord'', chapter 16</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|[[Daji]] questions if everyone in the [[Akura]] family is insane.<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c19">''Underlord'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|The [[Akura clan]] is mentioned in relation to [[Harmony]].<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c21">''Underlord'', chapter 21</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|The [[Akura clan]] owns territory where [[Lindon]]'s family lives.<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c21" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|[[Lindon]] will compete on behalf of the [[Akura clan]].<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c21" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|The favored young Underlords of the [[Akura clan]] are lined up.<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c22">''Underlord'', chapter 22</ref>}}

== Uncrowned ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|Composed of family members including a [[Monarch]] and a [[Fury]], the [[Akura]] clan is a powerful family led by the daughter of [[Monarch Malice]]. While they prepare [[Lindon]] and [[Mercy]] for the [[Uncrowned King]] tournament, [[Lindon]] remains a guest who was not raised with their specific training. The clan, which utilizes the [[Frozen Blade school]] as a vassal faction and displays a symbol of three stars over a moon-pale mountain range, provides high-quality clothing and sends gifts in ornate black wood chests. During the tournament, servants in rainbow robes usher them into the [[Ninecloud Court]], though the clan is targeted by gold [[dragons]] and eventually split apart during the second round of competition. Despite [[Eithan]] viewing them as oppressors, [[Lindon]] intends to gather the clan's competitors together in the jungle, and many members of the family attempt to speak with him.}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|The [[Akura clan]] is preparing [[Lindon]] and [[Mercy]] for the [[Uncrowned King]] tournament.<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c2">''Uncrowned'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|The [[Akura clan]] is composed of family members including a [[Monarch]] and [[Fury]].<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c3">''Uncrowned'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Lindon]] was not raised with the training of the [[Akura clan]].<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c3" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Lindon]] is considered a guest of the [[Akura]] family.<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c3" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|The [[Akura clan]] is a [[Monarch]]'s family.<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c4">''Uncrowned'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|The [[Akura clan]] sent a gift in an ornate black wood chest.<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c4" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|The [[Akura]] family symbol is a moon-pale image of a mountain range topped by three stars.<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c4" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|The [[Frozen Blade school]] is a vassal faction under the [[Akura clan]].<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c4" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Lindon]] stated he would not oppose the [[Akura clan]].<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c5">''Uncrowned'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|The [[Akura clan]] offers high-quality clothing.<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c8">''Uncrowned'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|The [[Akura clan]] was targeted in the tournament by gold [[dragons]].<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c9">''Uncrowned'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|The [[Akura clan]] is ushered into the [[Ninecloud Court]] by servants in rainbow robes.<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c9" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|The [[Akura]] faction is gathering together in the jungle.<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c13">''Uncrowned'', chapter 13</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|The [[Akura]] family is viewed by [[Eithan]] as oppressors.<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c14">''Uncrowned'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Lindon]] intends to gather [[Akura]] faction competitors.<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c14" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|The second round of the competition split the [[Akura]] faction.<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c15">''Uncrowned'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|The [[Akura]] family is led by the daughter of [[Monarch Malice]].<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c16">''Uncrowned'', chapter 16</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|Many people trying to talk to [[Lindon]] were from the [[Akura]] family.<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c21">''Uncrowned'', chapter 21</ref>}}

== Wintersteel ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|Residing in an amethyst spire within their assigned tower in [[Ninecloud City]], the purple-eyed [[Akura]] clan is a powerful faction associated with [[House Arelius]]. The clan possesses thousands of [[soulfire]] arts and has successfully raised a [[Herald]], a [[Sage]], and a [[Monarch]], maintaining alliances with both [[Sages]] and [[Heralds]]. While [[Lindon]] was previously taken to the family for training and later purchased [[hunger madra]] in their name, the clan's primary hope for winning [[Penance]] rests with [[Yerin]]. To support her, they have contributed advancement resources and gifted Sophara a [[life]]-size goldsteel statue of [[Ekeri]]. Despite facing chaos, a state of war, and hostility from ocean inhabitants, the clan remains capable of operating independently during crises, with members named after virtues and a reputation that exceeds any individual member.}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Akura clan]] has an assigned tower in [[Ninecloud City]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c3">''Wintersteel'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Akura]] family resides in an amethyst spire.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c3" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|Members of the [[Akura clan]] have purple eyes.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c3" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|[[Lindon]] was previously taken to the [[Akura]] family for training.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c5">''Wintersteel'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|[[Mercy]] can join the ordinary audience of the [[Akura]] faction.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c6">''Wintersteel'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|[[Lindon]] purchased [[hunger madra]] in the name of the [[Akura]] family.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c9">''Wintersteel'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The inhabitants of the ocean are largely hostile to the [[Akura clan]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c9" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Akura]] family has allies among the [[Heralds]] and [[Sages]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c9" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Akura]] family has raised a [[Herald]], a [[Sage]], and a [[Monarch]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c9" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Seishen Kingdom]] arrived at the location to obey an [[Akura]] summons.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c9" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Akura clan]] is a group mentioned by [[Yan Shoumei]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c11">''Wintersteel'', chapter 11</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|Some displaced [[sacred artists]] have sought shelter with the [[Akura clan]] factions.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c12">''Wintersteel'', chapter 12</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|[[Maraan]] intended to teach the [[Akura clan]] a lesson.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c14">''Wintersteel'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Akura clan]] had taken [[Lindon]] to task over [[Harmony]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c14" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Akura clan]]'s hope for winning [[Penance]] is [[Yerin]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c18">''Wintersteel'', chapter 18</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Akura clan]] possesses thousands of [[soulfire]] arts.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c19">''Wintersteel'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Akura clan]] gave Sophara a [[life]]-size goldsteel statue of [[Ekeri]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c20">''Wintersteel'', chapter 20</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Akura clan]] contributed advancement resources to [[Yerin]]'s training.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c20" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Akura]] name is greater than any one of its members.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c22">''Wintersteel'', chapter 22</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Akura clan]] is associated with [[House Arelius]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c22" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Akura clan]] is capable of operating on its own during a crisis.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c24">''Wintersteel'', chapter 24</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Akura]] family will provide resources to [[Yerin]] because their prosperity is in her hands.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|Every member of the [[Akura]] family is named after a virtue.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c27">''Wintersteel'', chapter 27</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Akura]] faction is handling their own war.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c27" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Akura]] faction was in a state of chaos.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c28">''Wintersteel'', chapter 28</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Akura clan]] settles down and may still honor contribution points.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c28" />}}

== Bloodline ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Akura]] clan, which is governed by a head family and based in their capital of [[Moongrave]], operates through three pillars. While the clan is known for providing protection to people, they faced scrutiny when [[Lindon]] blamed them for failing to assist those in [[Sacred Valley]]. During a period of investigation, the clan's strength was noted as having fallen from three stars to two. To assist with evacuations, the clan deployed a fleet of cloudships that unloaded refugees at [[Heaven's Glory]] School. During these operations, forty [[Akura]] clan [[Golds]] supported [[Lindon]], though the first waves of their forces had not yet returned.}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Akura clan]] has three pillars.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c2">''Bloodline'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|A representative of the [[Akura clan]] has arrived.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c2" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Akura clan]] provides protection to people.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c2" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Akura clan]] is conducting an investigation.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c3">''Bloodline'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Akura clan]] has three stars, but is down to two.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c3" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Lindon]] blames the [[Akura clan]] for not trying to help the people in [[Sacred Valley]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c4">''Bloodline'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Akura clan]] would not let the victor of the [[Uncrowned King]] tournament go without a [[void key]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c6">''Bloodline'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Akura clan]] has provided a fleet of cloudships for evacuation.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c9">''Bloodline'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Akura clan]] unloaded refugees at the [[Heaven's Glory]] School.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c10">''Bloodline'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|There are forty [[Akura clan]] [[Golds]] behind [[Lindon]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c11">''Bloodline'', chapter 11</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The first waves of the [[Akura clan]] have not yet returned.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c12">''Bloodline'', chapter 12</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Akura clan]] has a member who shouted to [[Lindon]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c12" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Akura clan]] has [[Golds]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c13">''Bloodline'', chapter 13</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Akura clan]] has a head family.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c13" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Moongrave]] is the capital of the [[Akura clan]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c17">''Bloodline'', chapter 17</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Jai Chen]] considered the [[Akura clan]] but did not know them.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c21">''Bloodline'', chapter 21</ref>}}

== Reaper ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The [[Akura]] clan, a powerful group ruled by [[Queen]] [[Malice]], maintains territory that includes the area where [[Dross]] speaks and features [[Herald]]-level spirits that shine like bonfires. After [[Lindon]] and [[Eithan]] previously borrowed the clan's name, the [[Akura]] declared the [[labyrinth]] territory off-limits and [[Lindon]] agreed to contact them if [[Mercy]]'s [[life]] were ever in danger. While [[Charity]] and [[Malice]] serve as pillars of the family, the [[Heart Sage]] took [[Mercy]] to assist in leading the clan. [[Lindon]] was eventually honored before the clan, and a statue of [[Mercy]] now sits at the head of the [[Akura]] in the [[World of Night]].}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Lindon]] and [[Eithan]] previously had to borrow the name of the [[Akura clan]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c4">''Reaper'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Lindon]] would contact the [[Akura clan]] if [[Mercy]]'s [[life]] were in danger.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c4" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The [[Akura clan]] declared the [[labyrinth]] territory off-limits.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c4" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Lindon]] was honored in front of the [[Akura clan]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c5">''Reaper'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Charity]] and [[Malice]] are pillars of the [[Akura]] family.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c7">''Reaper'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The [[Heart Sage]] took [[Mercy]] to help lead the [[Akura clan]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c7" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The area where [[Dross]] is speaking is [[Akura clan territory]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c10">''Reaper'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The [[Akura clan]] has [[Herald]]-level spirits that shine like bonfires.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c10" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The [[Akura clan]] is a clan ruled by [[Queen]] [[Malice]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c24">''Reaper'', chapter 24</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|A statue of [[Mercy]] sits at the head of the [[Akura clan]] in the [[World of Night]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c26">''Reaper'', chapter 26</ref>}}

== Dreadgod ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Akura]] clan is a formidable power that can be antagonized, maintaining influence through the allegiance of various [[Heralds]] and [[Sages]]. Represented by a purple-and-black banner, the clan possesses enough experts to keep [[Lindon]] under control and includes members such as [[Akura Justice]]. During the defense of [[Moongrave]], the clan mustered twelve Archlords to protect the area. Furthermore, [[Akura Malice]] has been known to speak with the full authority of the clan.}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|Some [[Sages]] and [[Heralds]] owe allegiance to the [[Akura clan]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c6">''Dreadgod'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Akura Justice]] is part of the [[Akura clan]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c8">''Dreadgod'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Akura clan]] has enough experts to keep [[Lindon]] under control.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c8" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Akura clan]] is a power that can be antagonized.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c10">''Dreadgod'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Akura clan]] uses a purple-and-black aesthetic as its banner.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c12">''Dreadgod'', chapter 12</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Akura clan]] mustered twelve Archlords for the defense of [[Moongrave]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c15">''Dreadgod'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Akura Malice]] spoke by the authority of the [[Akura clan]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c24">''Dreadgod'', chapter 24</ref>}}

== Waybound ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Lindon]] has not let [[Mercy]] out of his sight since leaving the [[Akura clan]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c2">''Waybound'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Mercy]] felt a memory of her faith in the [[Akura clan]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c13">''Waybound'', chapter 13</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Northstrider]] does not share the bias against [[sacred beasts]] held by many in the [[Akura clan]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c23">''Waybound'', chapter 23</ref>}}

== Threshold: Stories from Cradle ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[King Dakata]] was once a guest of the [[Akura]].<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c9">''Threshold: Stories from Cradle'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|The characters are currently in the [[Akura clan]]'s territory.<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c13">''Threshold: Stories from Cradle'', chapter 13</ref>}}

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-05-ghostwater|“We are here on behalf of the Blackflame Empire,” Renfei said loudly, “under the protection of the Akura family.”<ref name="cradle-05-ghostwater-c3" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-05-ghostwater|“We have no grudge against the Akura clan.<ref name="cradle-05-ghostwater-c18" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|“We were very careful not to do anything that might weaken fellow servants of the Akura clan,” King Dakata assured her.<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c16" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|Instead,” the Sage continued, "you will compete on behalf of the great Akura clan.<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c21" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|“By the authority of the Akura clan, let it be known that the sacred artist formerly known as Wei Shi Lindon Arelius has been corrupted by the power of the Silent King,” she announced.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c24" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{Cradle nav}}

[[Category:Groups]]
[[Category:Skysworn]]
[[Category:Ghostwater]]
[[Category:Underlord]]
[[Category:Uncrowned]]
[[Category:Wintersteel]]
[[Category:Bloodline]]
[[Category:Reaper]]
[[Category:Dreadgod]]
[[Category:Waybound]]
[[Category:Threshold: Stories from Cradle]]
[[Category:Cradle]]
[[Category:Cradle groups]]
